name: Circular by the west of Parque Natural Arribes del Duero — by the Salto de Villalcampo
name_original: Circular por el oeste del Parque Natural Arribes del Duero — por el Salto de Villalcampo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villalcampo offer diverse and scenic landscapes, characterized by dramatic canyons carved by the Douro River and the presence of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. The region features a mix of challenging terrain and picturesque routes, including views of the impressive Ricobayo Dam on the Esla River. Cyclists can expect well-paved surfaces and varied topography, providing opportunities for exploration. Best road cycling routes around Villalcampo The most popular road cycling route is Miranda do Douro and Arribes del Duero Loop, a 37.9 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular by the west of Parque Natural Arribes del Duero — by the Salto de Villalcampo, a moderate 21.9 miles (35.2 km) path. This route provides views of the natural park and the Villalcampo Hydroelectric Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Ricobayo Reservoir Loop, a 60.8 miles (97.9 km) trail leading through the Ricobayo Reservoir area, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain. Road cycling around Villalcampo is defined by the dramatic canyons of the Douro River, the Ricobayo Dam, and the varied topography of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Villalcampo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 190 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villalcampo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Villalcampo?
A: Villalcampo offers a diverse range of road cycling routes suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult routes. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025443287/miranda-do-douro-and-arribes-del-duero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda do Douro and Arribes del Duero Loop&lt;/a&gt; are considered moderate, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025434799/ricobayo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; presents a more difficult challenge with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while road cycling in Villalcampo?
A: Road cycling routes around Villalcampo are rich in natural beauty and impressive landmarks. You can expect dramatic canyons carved by the Douro River, especially within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Notable sights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; on the Esla River, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São João das Arribas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views of the Douro River.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a road cycling tour in Villalcampo?
A: Yes, the region boasts several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São João das Arribas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, located in the Douro International Natural Park, provides breathtaking panoramic views of the Douro River and its dramatic rock formations. Another excellent spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Hullón&lt;/a&gt;, offering more spectacular vistas of the canyons.

Q: Can I see waterfalls on road cycling routes in Villalcampo?
A: While road cycling routes primarily offer views of the river canyons and reservoirs, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a popular natural treasure in the Sayago region, within the Zamora slope of the Arribes del Duero. Some routes might pass near access points, but it's typically a short detour or hike to reach the waterfall itself.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes available in Villalcampo?
A: Yes, Villalcampo offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369343944/circular-by-the-west-of-parque-natural-arribes-del-duero-by-the-salto-de-villalcampo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular by the west of Parque Natural Arribes del Duero — by the Salto de Villalcampo&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views of the natural park and the Villalcampo Hydroelectric Reservoir. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025443287/miranda-do-douro-and-arribes-del-duero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda do Douro and Arribes del Duero Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villalcampo?
A: The diverse topography around Villalcampo makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are milder but can have cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Villalcampo?
A: Yes, Villalcampo and the surrounding towns typically offer parking options for visitors. Many routes start from or pass through villages where you can find suitable places to park your vehicle before beginning your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Villalcampo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic canyons of the Douro River, the impressive Ricobayo Dam, and the varied topography of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park. Cyclists appreciate the well-paved surfaces and the options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369331615/zamora-and-sayago-region-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora and Sayago Region Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover over 100 km with significant elevation gain, offering a strenuous and rewarding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Villalcampo?
A: While the routes themselves traverse natural and rural landscapes, you will typically find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the villages and towns you pass through or start from. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, to ensure you have refreshment options.

Q: Are there any historical engineering marvels visible from the routes?
A: Yes, the region features notable engineering structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3409877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requejo Bridge&lt;/a&gt; (Pino Bridge) over the Duero River is an historical engineering marvel within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, connecting the Aliste and Sayago regions. It's a testament to early 20th-century construction and often visible from cycling paths.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villalcampo?
A: There are over 18 road cycling routes around Villalcampo, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's unique landscapes.
tips:
The Requejo Bridge, also known as the Pino Bridge, is a jewel of historical engineering located in Villadepera (Zamora) over the Duero River, within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

Year of inauguration: 1914.
Designed by the engineer José Eugenio Ribera, a pioneer in the use of reinforced concrete in Spain.

It was built to connect the Aliste and Sayago regions, facilitating communication between Zamora and Portugal.

In this town that is located on one of the horns of one of the meanders created by the Río Duero itself, at the Villalcampo Dam we find its church. Interesting village that falls to the same dam in a circuit of open curves with views of the valley carved by the same river.
